---
title: "FW 536 — Day 1 afternoon lab (v3 · terrestrial wildlife & disease) — Your Name"
author: "Your Name Here"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    self_contained: true
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    toc_float: true
    code_folding: show
    theme: cosmo
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
```

# Day 1 afternoon lab — Discrete distributions problem set

**Problem set version:** **v3 — terrestrial wildlife & disease theme**.
(The companion version is [v2 — marine & fisheries](problem_set_v2.html); you choose whichever theme fits your interests.)

This template matches [`problem_set_v3.html`](problem_set_v3.html). Read each problem statement there, write your work below, **Knit** to HTML, and upload the `.html` to the matching Canvas assignment.

For setup help see the [R Markdown tutorial](../rmarkdown_tutorial.html).

---

# Setup

```{r}
# Load packages you'll need. Add or remove as appropriate.
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
```

---

# Problem 6 — Owl pellet contents

> Barn owl pellet contents from a regional study are composed of voles 0.65, shrews 0.20, mice 0.10, birds 0.05. A pellet contains 15 identifiable prey items.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 7 — Coyote scat counts on grid cells

> Coyote scats per grid cell from a recent survey have mean 4 and variance 28.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 8 — Brucellosis in Yellowstone bison

> Hypothetical herd prevalence is 0.15. You sample $n$ bison. You want at least 95% probability of detecting at least one positive bison.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 9 — Solitary cougars in a linear gradient

> $n=30$ cougar territories are arranged linearly along a ridgeline. Each cougar independently has probability $p=0.3$ of "calling tonight." Count the expected number of isolated callers — a cougar that calls, but neither immediate neighbor calls. Consider only the 28 interior c...

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 10 — Grizzly bear DNA from hair snares

> A hair snare grid has 40 stations. The probability of getting at least one usable hair sample at a station in one occasion is $p = 0.18$ when a bear is present. Bears are present at 60% of stations.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# (Optional) Embed a photo of hand-written work

For problems easier to do on paper, photograph and embed:

```{r, echo=FALSE, eval=FALSE, out.width="60%", fig.cap="Hand-derivation for Problem N"}
knitr::include_graphics("images/problemN_derivation.jpg")
```

(Change `eval=FALSE` to `eval=TRUE` after saving an image. See the [R Markdown tutorial](../rmarkdown_tutorial.html) for details.)

---

# Submission checklist

- [ ] Answered every afternoon problem from `problem_set_v3.html`.
- [ ] Knitted to HTML without errors.
- [ ] Photos (if any) embedded with `self_contained: true` in the YAML.
- [ ] Uploaded the `.html` to the matching Canvas assignment.
